text: Burgers bore interacting with a KdV soliton in coupled Burgers-swept-KdV: does the soliton transmit, reflect, fuse, or get destroyed by T=8.0?

ic: u(x,0)=1.5*(1-tanh(x/0.5))/2 (smoothed bore u_L=1.5,u_R=0); v(x,0)=1.5*sech^2(x+8) (KdV soliton at x=-8)
method: Fourier pseudospectral spatial derivatives (no dealiasing), classical explicit RK4 in time; full RHS treated explicitly including v_xxx
bank_use_rationale: E1 must be a simplest baseline per progressive-complexity discipline. I avoid central FD per kb-burgers-fwdEuler-centralFD-Gibbs and kb-general-centralFD-hyperbolic-shockFormation: pseudospectral derivatives are the cleanest 'no-upwinding, no-limiter' baseline that still resolves v_xxx without requiring O(dx^3) timestep. I deliberately omit dealiasing at E1 so that if aliasing is the binding wall (per BKdV-S1 negative kb), it shows up as the observable failure mode. RK4 is explicit textbook time-stepping with no IMEX, no splitting. dt=1e-4 satisfies textbook v_xxx CFL 1.47e-4 for k_max=pi/dx with margin.

rationale: Classic aliasing-driven overflow: pseudospectral RK4 without 2/3-rule dealiasing forces quadratic-product modes (v^2, u*v, v*v_x) to wrap above Nx/2 and feed back into resolved band with no dissipation. Matches BKdV-S1 deep-synthesis kb signature (overflow before t<1). dt=1e-4 is below v_xxx CFL, so dispersion-stiffness is NOT the binding wall.

Single-component upgrade per progressive-complexity rule: add 2/3-rule spectral dealiasing on every nonlinear product. Same Fourier+RK4 stack, same dt=1e-4. This isolates whether aliasing was the sole binding wall (positive expected outcome per BKdV-S1).

method: Fourier pseudospectral + 2/3-rule dealiasing on every nonlinear product (v^2, u*v, v*v_x, u*u_x) + classical explicit RK4
bank_use_rationale: BKdV-S1 deep-synthesis confirms: Fourier + 2/3-dealias + classical RK4 at dt<=2e-4 reaches T=10 cleanly with mass conserved <1e-12; effective k_max=(2/3)pi/dx raises RK4 v_xxx CFL bound from 1.47e-4 to 4.94e-4, so dt=1e-4 is safe. I reject IFRK4 (kb-kdv-IFRK4-blowup, overflow at high k) and explicit RK4 + central FD (kb-kdv-explicit-RK4-stiffness-blowup, fragments at dt=1e-5). No dealias is rejected (kb-kdv-noDealiasing-aliasing-artifacts, F1 reproduced this). LxF rejected (kb-burgers-LaxFriedrichs-longTime-dissipation: over-damps bore). Single-component change vs E1: add dealiasing mask. No IMEX, no splitting yet.

rationale: Phenomenon target MET. v retains a single coherent peak with amplitude 0.665 >= 0.5 (soliton survived); u_max=3.46 < 5 (bore bounded, did not blow up). Both masses conserved to machine precision (<1e-14). The bore steepens as expected (max|u| rises 1.5->3.46) consistent with Burgers shock formation while the soliton interacts. v amplitude oscillates 1.5->0.6 during interaction and ends at 0.66 — refraction/partial transmission regime. No NaN, no spurious peak proliferation. BKdV-S1 deep-synthesis positive transfer confirmed at T=8.

Phenomenon target satisfied on the FIRST single-component upgrade over E1 baseline. Adding further complexity (IMEX, MUSCL-Godunov) is unjustified — would violate progressive-complexity rule that each Experiment must address the previously observed failure mode. F1 failure mode (aliasing) is fully addressed by 2/3 dealiasing in E2. Stop early; E2 is the final answer.
